findings.evidence: The HTML rendering presents a correctly formatted visual layout of the page, but then appends an unformatted, plain-text version of the entire page content at the bottom. A user reading top-to-bottom would read the letter twice.
Similar to page 1, the HTML presents the visual layout followed by a plain-text dump of the page's content at the bottom.

findings.description: Incorrect reading order across page boundaries in text section
findings.evidence: In the duplicated plain-text section at the bottom of the page, the text flow begins with the footer content from Page 1 ('North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality...') before displaying the header for Page 2.
